title: Raise governance decision path coverage

sources.reason: Raises floors for governance decision modules after targeted coverage is added.
Covers checkpoint, capability, and required-gate rejection paths.
Covers scenario risk and explicit acknowledgment branches.
Contains guard calibration and governance integration fixtures.

riskAssessment.reason: Tests and coverage floors change only verification strength, not runtime behavior.

executionDecision.reason: Existing coverage reports and focused test modules define the implementation boundary.

preReviewWarnings: Review that raised floors correspond to meaningful decision and failure-path tests rather than incidental line coverage.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Focused tests cover the selected agent-risk, scenario-coverage, and guard-calibration decision and failure paths.
Critical coverage floors for ai_check_agent_risk, ai_check_guard_calibration, and ai_check_scenario_coverage are raised to at least 75%.
The full project quality gate and AI Cockpit checks pass without changing runtime governance behavior.
guidelines: Raise floors only after targeted decision-path evidence exists.
Keep the change limited to tests and coverage policy; do not weaken existing gates.

intent.problem: Three governance decision modules remain only around 65%–69% covered despite their fail-closed role.
constraints: Do not alter runtime behavior to improve coverage.
Do not lower any existing coverage threshold.
intent.rationale: More direct evidence on rejection and recovery paths reduces the risk of silent governance regressions.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
